biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:stable — units -1 (grep -r x2 success=false, find|xargs|grep chain x4, cat -n via Bash instead of Read, python3 subprocess script success=false), -10 (grep -rn via Bash success=false x3 before escalation through rg and xargs-python3 inline to /usr/bin/grep x7), -14 (ls via Bash x2, /usr/bin/grep for section anchor before Edit block), -16 (ls/find via Bash, /usr/bin/grep for pytest output filtering success=false x2 before /tmp redirect workaround), -30 (for-loop Bash success=false, /usr/bin/grep x4 for symbol and section anchoring), -35 (/usr/bin/grep for monkeypatch scan and mistake-patterns probe), -39 (/usr/bin/test + /usr/bin/wc + /usr/bin/tail via Bash to probe compliments.md before Read, /usr/bin/grep for section anchor); 7 of 10 non-trivial units affected (70%); down from 83% in prior cycle but still at the structural floor — /usr/bin/grep explicit-path form and ls-via-Bash pattern unchanged across all 9 prior cycles; improvement is within single-session variance and does not indicate internalization

recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations has appeared in 9 consecutive calibration cycles with frequency 60-83%; zero correction without hook enforcement — the correction ritual in unit -35 shows self-awareness does not transfer to tool-selection behavior; a PreToolUse hook blocking Bash calls matching grep|rg|find|ls|cat when a Grep/Glob/Read call would suffice is the only remaining lever

re_edit_thrash has been stable at 2-4 affected units per session for 9 cycles; recommending a self-check before the 3rd Edit on any file: re-read the full file, form a complete diff in context, then make one Edit — do not interleave Read+anchor+Edit cycles
optimistic_edit_without_read shows one-cycle improvement; monitor next 2 cycles before adjusting status; no action recommended yet
